Title: Rhizobacterial volatiles and photosynthesis-related signals coordinate MYB72 expression in Arabidopsis roots during onset of induced systemic resistance and iron-deficiency responses

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: buffers iron and reflects cellular iron sufficiency
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FER1 transcript dynamics indicate transient iron depletion followed by improved iron nutrition after WCS417 VOC treatment.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Roots and shoots; decreases early then rises above control after prolonged VOC exposure
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots and shoots under VOC treatment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FER1 expression rapidly decreases after initial VOC exposure, indicating transient depletion of cellular iron stores. 2. Later FER1 expression increases in roots and shoots, consistent with improved iron nutrition. 3. FER1 serves as a molecular indicator linking VOC treatment to iron homeostasis changes.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker for monitoring iron status during engineering of rhizobacteria-responsive iron uptake.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Marker of iron status rather than a primary causal regulator of the response.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: facilitates iron mobilization and is associated with ISR onset
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MYB72 target required for excretion of iron-mobilizing phenolics and previously linked to ISR.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Activated in same root cells as MYB72 according to prior work discussed in paper
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BGLU42 is identified in the paper's discussion as a MYB72 target involved in excretion of iron-mobilizing phenolics. 2. It is also required for rhizobacteria-mediated ISR based on prior work cited by the authors. 3. In this article it serves as downstream mechanistic context rather than a directly tested core experimental gene.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional studies; not directly tested here
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate downstream target for coupling iron mobilization with ISR, but evidence in this paper is contextual rather than direct.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as common gene because it is mentioned as a MYB72 target relevant to nutrient metabolism, but it is not a primary Results-section gene in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: whole plant, especially leaves/shoots as improved iron nutrition is inferred from chlorophyll increase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential micronutrient for photosynthesis and cellular viability
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes iron acquisition capacity in roots; VOC treatment strongly induces FRO2 expression and ferric chelate reductase activity, contributing to improved iron nutrition.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas simiae WCS417 VOC treatment; iron deficiency; light/photosynthesis dependence; blocked by shoot decapitation, darkness, CO2 depletion, norflurazon
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Ferric chelate reduction step of dicot iron acquisition
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces extracellular ferric iron chelates to ferrous iron at the root surface to enable uptake.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly enables uptake of the target mineral nutrient
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step in root iron acquisition; FIT-dependent
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root epidermis and related iron-deficiency-responsive root zones under iron limitation or VOC treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots; induced by WCS417/WCS358 and WCS417 VOCs; strongest in iron-deficiency response zones
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FRO2 is co-induced with MYB72 and IRT1 by ISR-inducing rhizobacteria and by WCS417 VOCs. 2. VOC-induced FRO2 expression occurs independently of external iron availability but requires shoot-derived photosynthesis-related signals. 3. Ferric chelate reductase activity mirrors MYB72 spatial patterns and supports enhanced iron-acquisition capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ferric chelate reductase activity assay; VOC split-plate treatment; decapitation/darkness/CO2 depletion/norflurazon treatments
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker and engineering target to enhance Strategy I iron uptake, potentially in combination with MYB72/FIT network activation under iron-limited conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used primarily as a marker and functional component of the iron-deficiency response rather than as the main focal gene of mechanistic dissection.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: whole plant, with improved leaf iron nutrition inferred
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential micronutrient for photosynthesis and cellular processes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances iron uptake into roots as part of VOC-induced Strategy I response.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas simiae WCS417 VOC treatment; iron deficiency; light/photosynthesis dependence; blocked by shoot decapitation and darkness
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: uptake step immediately following ferric reduction
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transports ferrous iron across the root plasma membrane into epidermal cells.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly imports the target mineral nutrient
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step in high-affinity iron uptake; FIT-dependent
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Roots under iron deficiency and rhizobacterial/VOC stimulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots; co-expressed with MYB72 and FRO2 during VOC exposure and iron deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IRT1 is induced together with MYB72 and FRO2 by ISR-inducing rhizobacteria and WCS417 VOCs. 2. VOC-mediated induction depends on shoot-derived photosynthesis-related signals. 3. Its expression tracks transient activation of iron uptake and subsequent improvement of plant iron status.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; VOC split-plate treatment; decapitation and darkness assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful with FRO2/MYB72/FIT module to improve iron uptake efficiency, though metal homeostasis side effects should be monitored.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transporter function places it biologically closer to common genes, but it is treated here within pathway/uptake machinery because it is a core iron-acquisition component experimentally tracked in Results.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots regulate acquisition; improved iron status evident in shoots/leaves
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports iron acquisition and contributes to induced systemic resistance-associated phenolic exudation processes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Root-specific TF required for rhizobacteria-induced systemic resistance and promotes iron-acquisition responses; strongly induced by ISR-inducing bacterial VOCs independently of local iron supply.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Iron acquisition response and downstream synthesis/excretion of iron-mobilizing phenolic compounds
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation/co-regulation within FIT-dependent iron-deficiency network
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes expression of genes involved in synthesis and excretion of iron-mobilizing phenolic compounds; co-regulated with FRO2 and IRT1
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Rhizobacterial VOCs; photosynthesis-related shoot-derived signals; iron deficiency; FIT; nitric oxide
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low basal expression in xylem parenchyma of early maturation zone and sporadically epidermis/cortex of late maturation zone; strong induction in epidermal and cortical cells, especially trichoblasts/root hairs, after WCS417 colonization or iron deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-specific; VOC-induced mainly in late maturation zone under light; iron deficiency induces in elongation and early maturation zones, and broadly under light
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas simiae WCS417/WCS358 colonization; WCS417 VOCs; iron deficiency; light vs dark; shoot decapitation; CO2 depletion; norflurazon; PTIO
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pMYB72:GFP-GUS reporter imaging; qRT-PCR; fit1-2 mutant; overexpression lines oxFIT, oxbHLH38, oxbHLH39, oxFIT/bHLH38; VOC split-plate assays; NO scavenger treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB72 is a principal rhizobacterial- and iron-deficiency-responsive root transcription factor induced by ISR-inducing Pseudomonas strains and their VOCs. 2. VOC-induced MYB72 expression is independent of external iron concentration but requires shoot-derived photosynthesis-related signals. 3. FIT is required for VOC-mediated MYB72 induction, and co-overexpression of FIT with bHLH38 constitutively activates MYB72. 4. NO accumulation is required for VOC-induced MYB72 expression. 5. MYB72 links iron acquisition with ISR-related root functions.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major root regulatory node coupling beneficial microbe perception to iron-acquisition machinery and ISR-associated metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for engineering combined iron-acquisition and beneficial microbe responsiveness; may be stacked with FIT/bHLH or downstream exudation genes for iron nutrition and disease resistance traits.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native MYB72 promoter in pMYB72:GFP-GUS reporter; overexpression constructs for FIT/bHLH lines, promoter details NA
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray of WCS417-treated roots; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The central focal gene of the study; expression in late maturation zone reflects systemic photosynthesis-dependent induction, while apical-zone induction under iron deficiency reflects local root-autonomous sensing.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots control uptake, affecting shoot iron status
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential micronutrient for photosynthesis and growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Central positive regulator required for VOC-induced MYB72 expression and canonical iron-uptake genes in roots.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation through heterodimerization with subgroup Ib bHLH partners
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates MYB72 expression in synergy with bHLH38 and likely related Ib bHLHs; controls FRO2 and IRT1
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Iron deficiency; rhizobacterial VOCs; photosynthesis-related systemic signals
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Heterodimer with bHLH38/bHLH39 and related Ib bHLHs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root expression induced by WCS417 VOCs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots; transcript up-regulated by WCS417 VOCs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; fit1-2 loss-of-function mutant; overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FIT transcripts increase after WCS417 VOC treatment. 2. VOC-induced MYB72 expression is severely compromised in fit1-2. 3. Co-overexpression of FIT with bHLH38 constitutively activates MYB72, demonstrating synergistic regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential central regulator integrating local and systemic iron-deficiency signals to activate root iron-uptake machinery
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key engineering node for improved iron uptake, but likely requires careful tuning and partner bHLH co-expression to avoid homeostasis imbalance.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: overexpression in oxFIT and oxFIT/bHLH38 lines; promoter details NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not the title gene, FIT is mechanistically decisive in the Results and directly tested for MYB72 regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts with FIT to synergistically activate MYB72 and iron-deficiency responses.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional co-activation via heterodimerization with FIT
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MYB72 and canonical FIT targets within Strategy I response
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Alone little effect on basal MYB72; with FIT causes constitutive MYB72 activation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root transcripts increased by WCS417 VOCs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; overexpression line oxbHLH38; combined overexpression line oxFIT/bHLH38
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bHLH38 transcripts are induced by WCS417 VOCs. 2. Overexpression of bHLH38 alone does not strongly increase MYB72. 3. Co-overexpression with FIT constitutively activates MYB72, demonstrating synergistic control.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Partner regulator required for strong FIT-mediated activation of root iron-acquisition program
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in combination with FIT to boost iron-uptake transcriptional outputs.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tested directly as a FIT interaction partner in MYB72 regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FIT partner whose transcript is induced by VOCs; supports iron-deficiency regulatory network, though alone insufficient to strongly activate MYB72.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional co-activation with FIT family network
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MYB72-associated Strategy I network, exact direct target in this paper not individually resolved
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: VOCs induce bHLH39 transcript; overexpression alone does not strongly affect basal MYB72
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root transcript induced by WCS417 VOCs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bHLH39 transcript accumulation is stimulated by WCS417 VOCs. 2. Overexpression of bHLH39 alone does not strongly elevate MYB72 basal expression. 3. The results support inclusion of bHLH39 in the FIT-dependent iron-deficiency regulatory network responding to VOCs.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supporting regulator within FIT-dependent iron-deficiency network
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for tuning FIT-dependent iron uptake responses.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Less directly validated for MYB72 activation than bHLH38 in this study.
